summary.intent: Implement task #267: the TreeView agnostic core component (plus DnD reorder variant) for the ars-ui Rust component library, following the spec at spec/components/navigation/tree-view.md.
summary.outcome: Full TreeView agnostic core implemented with 85 unit tests, 26 committed snapshots, spec-conformance, proptest invariants, 99% line/region coverage, and spec fully synced. Three audit findings were landed mid-session (disabled enforcement, controlled-prop sync, Key consistency). The mandatory pre-PR mutation gate was removed per user request; Nightly CI now owns mutant triage.

repo: TreeCollection::nodes() iterates visible_indices only — returns visible nodes, not all nodes. ExpandAll must use the new all_nodes() accessor to reach expandable nodes hidden under collapsed parents.
Key does not implement Borrow<str>, so spec-written String-based identity (.contains(id), set.insert(id) against BTreeSet<Key>) does not compile. The entire component family uses Key identity.

selection::State::select already rejects disabled keys internally, but disabled_keys must be explicitly seeded at init from TreeItem.disabled flags — omitting this silently allows disabled nodes to be selected.
Every component with controlled props implements on_props_changed to sync controlled values on re-render; omitting it means controlled updates and post-reorder item changes are silently dropped.
There is a blanket EffectName impl — components must not manually implement it. Doing so causes a conflicting-impl compile error.

Callback<T> implements Clone + Debug + PartialEq (pointer identity). The checkbox callback+effect pattern (on_foo: Option<Callback<...>> in Props, Effect::Foo emitted on transition) is the canonical wiring model.
KeyboardKey has no Character variant. Typed characters come from KeyboardEventData.character: Option<char>.
Navigation tests live in a single crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/navigation.rs file, not per-component splits. TreeView tests appended there.
spec-compile-snippets uses syn::parse_file for syntax checking only (no type resolution), so multi-block specs that reference types across blocks still pass as long as each block parses individually.
.agents/ and .claude/ skill files are hardlinked (same inode) — editing either updates both.
CLAUDE.md is a symlink to AGENTS.md — edits must target the real file.

The DraggableCollection/DroppableCollection trait impls, DOM hit-testing, hover-expand timers, and use_drag/use_drop wiring are adapter-layer concerns belonging to tasks #366 (Leptos) and #509 (Dioxus), not the agnostic core.
cargo-insta stops a test at its first unaccepted snapshot for multi-snapshot test functions; a separate UPDATE_EXPECT=1 or insta accept-all step is needed to generate the full set.

code.finding: visible_keys() lives in the impl<T: CollectionItem> block. Pure-read accessors without bounds (all_keys, all_nodes) belong in the no-bound impl<T> block at lines 134-175.
selection::State::select already rejects keys present in disabled_keys, but the set is only respected if it is populated at init time.

DropPosition, CollectionDropTarget, CollectionDndEvent, DraggableCollection/DroppableCollection traits, and CollectionDndMessages all live here. DraggableCollection::selection(&self) -> &selection::State is a required method, making it impossible for a bare collection to implement it without bundling live selection state.
Accordion provides a manual Debug impl for its Api struct (PhantomData in fields prevents auto-derive). This is the required pattern for all component Api types.

workflow: TDD was enforced: failing tests written before implementation for every non-trivial change, including the mid-session audit findings (disabled enforcement, prop sync, coverage gaps).
cargo xci --profile fast is the mandatory pre-push gate covering clippy -D warnings, tests, doc, spec validate, compile-snippets, insta --unreferenced=reject, and coverage check-all.
The mandatory pre-PR mutation gate for agnostic components was removed mid-session; surviving mutants are now triaged by Nightly CI rather than blocking the PR.
Parallel agent spawning (Explore subagents) for independent codebase lookups significantly reduced exploration time before implementation.
Snapshot tests must be explicitly accepted (UPDATE_EXPECT=1 or cargo insta accept) before they count as passing — they remain as pending .new files on first run.
Splitting the feature commit from a workflow-policy change commit within the same PR keeps the history auditable.
Stopping a background cargo-mutants run via TaskStop is clean — the tool releases the cargo lock and mutants.out/ accumulates partial results that are gitignored.

friction: The spec used String for node identity throughout (events, API params, BTreeSet), but the codebase convention and BTreeSet<Key> storage make String non-compilable and non-round-trippable for integer keys. Required a full spec and API contract rewrite before implementation could proceed.

TreeCollection::nodes() returning visible-only nodes is a silent pit: ExpandAll using it would compile and appear to work but silently skip collapsed subtrees. Required adding new all_nodes()/all_keys() accessors before the machine could be correct.

The spec referenced drag::Handle and drop::Indicator as part names, but the actual DnD infrastructure uses no shared utility parts — the correct names (DragHandle, DropIndicator) had to be reverse-engineered from sibling component patterns.
cargo-mutants buffers progress and does not write incrementally to mutants.out/ during a run, making interim polling unreliable. The only reliable signal is task completion.
A background mutation rerun was triggered after adding killer tests, then had to be stopped immediately after the user removed the mandatory gate — wasted ~36 min of compute.
CLAUDE.md being a symlink to AGENTS.md caused an initial failed edit that required resolving the real target path.

The spec's ExpandAll implementation using collection.nodes() was not just wrong but a latent silent bug — it would have compiled and passed unit tests using only visible trees, only failing with collapsed subtrees in integration.
open_items: Adapter-layer DnD for TreeView (DOM pointer/keyboard hit-testing, 500ms hover-expand timer, use_drag/use_drop wiring, DraggableCollection/DroppableCollection trait impls bundling live selection) deferred to task #366 (Leptos adapter) and #509 (Dioxus adapter).
Lazy Loading variant (spec §5) and Renamable Nodes variant (spec §6) are gated no_check blocks, explicitly out of scope for this task.
8 mutation survivors from the first gate run (6 missed + 2 timeouts): killer tests were added for all 8 but the confirmation rerun was stopped. These will be surfaced by Nightly CI.
83 pre-existing crate-wide rustdoc warnings (missing intra-doc links) were not addressed — they predate this change and are tracked separately.

The DropPosition::On reconciliation note left in tree-view.md spec §4.2 flags a naming inconsistency between the spec (Child) and implementation (On) that adapter tasks #366/#509 should resolve when wiring drop targets.
